rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports belong in component type scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annexes only in valid scopes matching component type or implementation.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope when generating AGREE contracts.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or resource constraints as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should appear after relevant subcomponents and connections in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL properties such as Period or Stack_Size as AGREE variables without model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: model repair and validator-driven minimal repair patterns
